_doc: Owner position rulings for THE DRAW real card set (Ticket E1.1).
WHY THIS FILE EXISTS. Each card below has exactly ONE Wikidata P413 statement:
"wing half". Wikidata's own P279 subclass tree says wing half ⊂ midfielder, so the
sourced position is MID and the ingest emits MID at GREEN quality — it is not a
conflict, there is nothing to flag. But "wing half" is a 1930s half-back role, and
Wikidata editors plainly use it as a catch-all for modern wingers: it sits on Neymar,
Bale, Ribéry, Di María, Figo and Mahrez. E1's conflict rule cannot help here — it
resolves BETWEEN statements, and these players have only one.
So the honest options are only two: ship the sourced MID, or have a human overrule
the source. Overruling it is a judgement call, not a fact, and this ticket forbids
silently overriding a FACT with an opinion. This file is how that judgement is made
explicit, attributable and auditable.
SCHEMA: { qid, name, nation, clubs, eraLabel, debutYear, sourcedStatement, sourcedPosition,
ruling: "MID" | "ATT" | null, note }
ruling  null => UNRULED => the card keeps the sourced MID. Absence of a ruling is
never read as agreement; it just means nobody has decided.
note    why. Read it before changing a ruling.
EFFECT. A ruling does NOT change playersSourced.json — the sourced fact stays MID
there, and the card dossier preserves the raw statement verbatim. It marks the card's
position `editorial-ruled`, attributed to the owner, exactly like rating and fameRank
are editorial. A ruling changes the position mix, so the selector re-balances after
applying it (see BUILD_NOTES).

rulings.name: Neymar
rulings.note: A forward throughout his career (Santos, Barcelona, PSG). played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.

rulings.name: Gareth Bale
rulings.note: Converted from left-back to a forward at Tottenham and played as a forward/winger at Real Madrid. played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.

rulings.name: Franck Ribéry
rulings.note: A winger at Marseille and Bayern for over a decade. played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.

rulings.name: Ángel Di María
rulings.note: A winger at Benfica, Real Madrid, PSG and Juventus. played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.

rulings.name: Riyad Mahrez
rulings.note: A right winger at Leicester and Manchester City. played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.

rulings.name: Luís Figo
rulings.note: A right winger at Barcelona, Real Madrid and Inter. played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.

rulings.name: Robinho
rulings.note: A forward/winger at Santos, Real Madrid, Manchester City and Milan. played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.

rulings.name: David Silva
rulings.note: An attacking midfielder at Valencia, Manchester City and Spain — genuinely a central midfielder — the sourced MID is right here, and the ruling confirms rather than overrides it.

rulings.name: Mario Götze
rulings.note: An attacking midfielder at Dortmund, Bayern and Germany — genuinely a central midfielder — the sourced MID is right here, and the ruling confirms rather than overrides it.

rulings.name: Nani
rulings.note: A winger at Sporting and Manchester United. played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.

rulings.name: Alexis Sánchez
rulings.note: A forward at Barcelona, Arsenal and Inter. played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.

rulings.name: Dirk Kuyt
rulings.note: A striker at Feyenoord and the Netherlands; deployed wide at Liverpool but known as a forward. played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.

rulings.name: Park Ji-sung
rulings.note: A central/wide midfielder at PSV and Manchester United — genuinely a central midfielder — the sourced MID is right here, and the ruling confirms rather than overrides it.

rulings.name: Antonio Valencia
rulings.note: Made his name as a right winger at Wigan and Manchester United (later converted to right-back). played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.

rulings.name: Ricardo Quaresma
rulings.note: A winger at Porto, Inter and Beşiktaş. played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.

rulings.name: Theo Walcott
rulings.note: A winger/forward at Arsenal and Everton. played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.

rulings.name: Clarence Seedorf
rulings.note: A central midfielder at Ajax, Real Madrid, Inter and Milan — genuinely a central midfielder — the sourced MID is right here, and the ruling confirms rather than overrides it.

rulings.name: Santi Cazorla
rulings.note: A central/attacking midfielder at Villarreal and Arsenal — genuinely a central midfielder — the sourced MID is right here, and the ruling confirms rather than overrides it.

rulings.name: Maxi Rodríguez
rulings.note: A midfielder at Atlético, Liverpool and Argentina — genuinely a central midfielder — the sourced MID is right here, and the ruling confirms rather than overrides it.

rulings.name: Lucas Vázquez
rulings.note: Made his name as a right winger at Real Madrid (later deployed at right-back). played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.

rulings.name: Denis Cheryshev
rulings.note: A winger at Valencia, Villarreal and Russia. played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.

rulings.name: Ole Gunnar Solskjær
rulings.note: A striker at Manchester United — the least ambiguous case in this file. played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.

rulings.name: Willian
rulings.note: A winger at Shakhtar, Chelsea and Arsenal. played as a winger/forward; the sole 'wing half' statement is the Wikidata catch-all for wingers, not a claim he played half-back.

rulings.name: Ibrahim Afellay
rulings.note: A midfielder at PSV, Barcelona and the Netherlands — genuinely a central midfielder — the sourced MID is right here, and the ruling confirms rather than overrides it.

rulings.name: Jairzinho
